Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

### Message

PRD to grow oracle-isolated mergeability calibration into a held-out task-class corpus with per-class controls, no-regression reporting, replayable hashes, and calibration-only authority flags. Public boundaries (validate_mergeability_corpus:474, run_paired_acceptance_pilot:592) are real; split/task_class/no_regression are genuinely absent from source (net-new); report-only authority substrate exists hardcoded False (metric_applyable:822, improvement_claim_allowed:823, applyable_policy_proposal:838). Grills 1-4 resolved, OOS clear. ACCEPT.

### Confidence

- value: `0.84`
- source: `claude_code_self_reported`
- rationale: PRD boundaries verified real in source, new fields verified absent (genuinely additive), authority substrate verified present, grills resolved and OOS clear. Held below 0.95 because PRD-stage evidence cannot run tests, and P4's cannot-flip promise is a preservation guarantee over pre-existing hardcoded flags rather than a new mechanism (low-severity quality note deferred to TDD gate).

Criteria:

- public boundaries exist in source
- new fields/controls genuinely absent (additive)
- report-only authority substrate present
- grill findings resolved
- out-of-scope disclaims applyability/live-gen

Evidence:

- accept

### Claims

- Public boundaries named in PRD (P5) exist in source
- P1/P2/P3 fields and per-class controls are net-new (additive), not already implemented
- P4 authority flags have a real substrate already hardcoded False
- No FM-1.3 step repetition: distinct handoff, new symbols absent from source

### Objections

- None recorded.

### Questions

- None recorded.

### Message

issues.md decomposes the held-out mergeability corpus PRD into 2 slices covering all 5 promises with no orphans; public boundaries (validate_mergeability_corpus:474, run_paired_acceptance_pilot:592, build_mergeability_corpus_manifest:393) are real and the described work (task_class/held_out/no_regression coverage, per-class control validation) is net-new vs current source. ACCEPT.

### Confidence

- value: `0.84`
- source: `claude_code_self_reported`
- rationale: Static trace confirms full PRD coverage, real boundaries, and genuine net-new RED basis; residual uncertainty is pre-implementation only (no pytest run, S2-AC3 hash scope mildly ambiguous, S2-AC2 is preservation not net-new). These are TDD-phase concerns and do not block the issues gate.

Criteria:

- all 5 PRD promises traced to slices with no orphans
- public boundary symbols exist in source
- described fields absent from current source (genuine RED)
- report-only authority substrate exists
- FM-1.3 not applicable

Evidence:

- accept

### Claims

- issues.md is a faithful, complete decomposition of the PRD with real public boundaries and a genuine RED basis
- All slice scopes describe net-new work except the flag-preservation guardrail in S2-AC2

### Objections

- low-sev: S2-AC2 (metric_applyable/improvement_claim_allowed/default_change_allowed/policy_mutated/gate_advanced all False) preserves an existing invariant - flags already hardcoded False at report:822-835 and validate:576-579 - rather than asserting net-new behavior
- low-sev: S2-AC3 deterministic hashes partly overlap existing report_sha256:842 and candidate_pool_sha256:776; net-new value is a dedicated task_class-coverage/no-regression hash, left mildly ambiguous
- low-sev: issues.md has no explicit reverse Coverage Index (PRD-promise to slice/test map); reviewer reconstructed it

### Questions

- None recorded.

## 42. TDD Review

- event_id: `816905`
- ts: `1781906426`
- interaction_type: `review_response`
- sender: `cursor`
- recipient: `codex`
- round_index: `1`
- persona_id: `cursor.independent_reviewer`
- addresses: `event:816794`

### Message

Independent Cursor Reviewer assessment of tdd_review gate. The TDD plan declares 5 tests covering PRD promises P1-P5 at public boundaries (run_paired_acceptance_pilot, validate_mergeability_corpus). Claude's outcome claims genuine RED status confirmed by static trace - task_class, no_regression, and held_out fields are absent from current source, and all 5 test names are absent. Planning artifacts (PRD, issues, TDD plan, grill findings, implementation plan) are present with consistent SHA256 hashes across duplicate path entries. No changed_files exist (base_head == candidate_head), which is expected for a TDD planning gate. No pytest execution evidence exists (executed_test_receipt_ids and runtime_receipt_ids are empty), which is acceptable at this gate since tests are designed to be RED by construction. The weakest tests are t4 (guard-preservation) and t5 (hash overlap with existing report_sha256), but both still represent genuine RED because the new report paths/fields don't exist yet. Gate should advance.

### Confidence

- value: `0.82`
- source: `cursor_self_reported`
- rationale: Planning artifacts are internally consistent with matching SHA256 hashes across duplicate paths. All 5 declared tests map to PRD promises P1-P5 with no orphans. RED genuineness is confirmed by the absence of task_class/no_regression/held_out fields and all 5 test names from current source. base_head == candidate_head confirms no implementation has occurred yet. Confidence held below 0.85 because: (1) no pytest execution evidence exists, (2) t4/t5 have partial overlap with existing mechanisms, and (3) I cannot independently verify the exact source file contents without file system access - I am relying on Claude's static trace claims.

Criteria:

- Planning artifacts present with consistent SHA256 hashes: PASS
- 5 declared tests map to PRD P1-P5 with no orphans: PASS
- RED genuineness (fields/test names absent from source): PASS (per Claude trace)
- Public boundary-first design (run_paired_acceptance_pilot, validate_mergeability_corpus): PASS
- Grill findings resolved (4 findings): PASS
- No changed_files (TDD planning gate): PASS (base==candidate)
- pytest execution evidence: NOT AVAILABLE (expected at this gate)
- Independent source verification: PARTIAL (relying on Claude's static trace)

Evidence:

- test_paired_report_records_heldout_task_class_coverage
- test_validate_mergeability_corpus_requires_controls_per_task_class
- test_paired_report_catches_no_regression_failure
- test_heldout_no_regression_report_remains_non_applyable
- test_no_regression_and_heldout_artifacts_export_replayable_hashes
- accept

### Claims

- All 5 tests target public boundaries (run_paired_acceptance_pilot, validate_mergeability_corpus)
- RED status is genuine: task_class, no_regression, held_out fields and all 5 test names absent from current source
- PRD promises P1-P5 are fully covered by the 5 tests with no orphan tests
- Grill findings (helper-only, vacuity, report-only, replayability risks) are resolved in the TDD plan
- No implementation has occurred yet (base_head == candidate_head), consistent with TDD planning gate

### Objections

- LOW: t4 and t5 have partial overlap with existing mechanisms (guard-preservation and report_sha256), but remain genuinely RED due to absent new fields/paths
- LOW: No pytest execution evidence, but this is expected at TDD planning gate where tests are RED by construction

### Questions

### Cursor Probe

- probe_id: `CURSOR`
- status: `green`
- reason: `cursor_review_ok`

### Cursor Outcome

Independent Cursor Reviewer assessment of tdd_review gate. The TDD plan declares 5 tests covering PRD promises P1-P5 at public boundaries (run_paired_acceptance_pilot, validate_mergeability_corpus). Claude's outcome claims genuine RED status confirmed by static trace - task_class, no_regression, and held_out fields are absent from current source, and all 5 test names are absent. Planning artifacts (PRD, issues, TDD plan, grill findings, implementation plan) are present with consistent SHA256 hashes across duplicate path entries. No changed_files exist (base_head == candidate_head), which is expected for a TDD planning gate. No pytest execution evidence exists (executed_test_receipt_ids and runtime_receipt_ids are empty), which is acceptable at this gate since tests are designed to be RED by construction. The weakest tests are t4 (guard-preservation) and t5 (hash overlap with existing report_sha256), but both still represent genuine RED because the new report paths/fields don't exist yet. Gate should advance.

Claims:

- All 5 tests target public boundaries (run_paired_acceptance_pilot, validate_mergeability_corpus)
- RED status is genuine: task_class, no_regression, held_out fields and all 5 test names absent from current source
- PRD promises P1-P5 are fully covered by the 5 tests with no orphan tests
- Grill findings (helper-only, vacuity, report-only, replayability risks) are resolved in the TDD plan
- No implementation has occurred yet (base_head == candidate_head), consistent with TDD planning gate

Decisions:

- accept

Objections:

- LOW: t4 and t5 have partial overlap with existing mechanisms (guard-preservation and report_sha256), but remain genuinely RED due to absent new fields/paths
- LOW: No pytest execution evidence, but this is expected at TDD planning gate where tests are RED by construction

Specialists:

- `Cursor Reviewer`: `accept` — objection: LOW: t4 (non-applyable) and t5 (replayable hashes) have partial overlap with existing guard/hash mechanisms, reducing net-new failure surface. However, both are genuinely RED because the new task_class-coverage and no_regression report fields are absent from current source. No blocking objection.

### Message

Supervisor-owned workflow gate: tdd_review.

Intent:
Grow the oracle-isolated mergeability calibration from a single fixture into a held-out task-class corpus with per-class controls, no-regression reporting, replayable hashes, and calibration-only authority flags.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Corrective context from the previous round:
independent_reviewer_non_accept: independent-reviewer-1

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
Use them as a checklist only: do not block, revise, deny, or accept solely because a lesson exists. A step-repetition lesson applies only when current evidence proves the same handoff, artifacts, and source state are being repeated.
1. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
2. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.
3. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=2a2338da-3ef5-4a6e-a794-7d0ce6006b51): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
4. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=2a2338da-3ef5-4a6e-a794-7d0ce6006b51): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.
5. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=bcf4a876-3308-4dd4-8c5b-b128b71d564a): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.

## 53. TDD Review

- event_id: `816948`
- ts: `1781906658`
- interaction_type: `gate_response`
- sender: `claude_code`
- recipient: `codex`
- round_index: `None`
- persona_id: `claude_code.lead_worker`
- addresses: `event:816919`, `handoff:/Users/sam.zhang/Documents/codex-supervisor/.handoff/mergeability-heldout-corpus-no-regression-20260619.json`

### Message

Re-gate of tdd_review after independent-reviewer-1 non-accept. tdd.md sha 6fd92da3 and source HEAD f8a0cca5 are byte-identical to the round I previously ACCEPTed, so re-emitting an identical ACCEPT would be step-repetition (FM-1.3 genuinely applies). RED is genuine and boundaries real (no DENY basis), so the honest outcome is REVISE: strengthen the two weakest tests (t4 GREEN-stays guard whose GREEN is already true; t5 hashes overlapping existing report_sha256:842).

### Confidence

- value: `0.8`
- source: `claude_code_self_reported`
- rationale: High confidence in the static evidence (HEAD unchanged, symbols/test-names Grep-0, boundaries real, sha match to prior ACCEPT). Confidence is below 0.9 because pytest was not run (tests absent by construction) and because the literal independent-reviewer-1 objection text is unavailable, so the revise target is the strongest inference rather than a confirmed quote.

Criteria:

- Source HEAD and tdd.md sha verified identical to prior ACCEPTed round
- RED genuineness confirmed by Grep-0 on test names and GREEN-target symbols
- Boundaries confirmed real and imported by the test module
- Weakest tests (t4/t5) re-examined against concrete source line numbers
- Independent-reviewer-1 objection text NOT available -> inference disclosed

Evidence:

- test_paired_report_records_heldout_task_class_coverage
- test_validate_mergeability_corpus_requires_controls_per_task_class
- test_paired_report_catches_no_regression_failure
- test_heldout_no_regression_report_remains_non_applyable
- test_no_regression_and_heldout_artifacts_export_replayable_hashes
- revise

### Claims

- RED is genuine for t1/t2/t3 and for the new fields of t4/t5 (underlying symbols absent at current source).
- Boundaries are real top-level functions, not helpers; test?PRD map has no orphans (t1->P1,P5; t2->P2,P5; t3->P3,P5; t4->P4,P5; t5->P1,P3,P4).
- No technical regression vs prior round; the only material change is the independent-reviewer non-accept on an otherwise-identical handoff.
- Revise is technically grounded (t4 GREEN already true; t5 hash overlap), not asserted solely because a failure-mode lesson exists.

### Objections

- FM-1.3 genuinely applies: handoff packet, tdd.md sha 6fd92da3, and source HEAD f8a0cca5 are identical to the prior round that independent-reviewer-1 rejected; re-emitting ACCEPT on unchanged evidence is prohibited step-repetition.
- t4 (test_heldout_no_regression_report_remains_non_applyable) GREEN assertion derive()==no-proposals is already true at current source (flags hardcoded False at report 822/823/833/834/835 and validate 576-579); its RED status rests solely on the absent held-out report path, not on driving a new guard.
- t5 (test_no_regression_and_heldout_artifacts_export_replayable_hashes) new replayable hashes overlap the existing report_sha256:842; plan does not pin them as per-finding hashes (task_class coverage + no_regression rows) provably distinct from the whole-report hash.
- Literal text of independent-reviewer-1 objection was not supplied in the packet; revise is framed around the strongest plausible target and the inference is disclosed (anti-FM-2.4).

### Questions

- None recorded.

### Critical Review

`{"assumptions_to_verify": ["That independent-reviewer-1's concern is about t4/t5 quality rather than a different defect (PRD coverage, boundary validity) - those were checked and found clean, raising likelihood the concern is t4/t5", "That a REVISE producing new artifact/evidence is an acceptable way to break the FM-1.3 repetition for this supervisor workflow"], "contradictions_checked": ["Whether source advanced since round 1 (it did not: HEAD f8a0cca5 unchanged)", "Whether GREEN targets already exist (they do not: task_class/no_regression/heldout Grep-0)", "Whether t4 GREEN behavior already passes at current source (yes: derive guard + flags already False -> supports the revise)", "Whether boundaries are helpers vs real public functions (real: 393/474/592, imported)"], "decision": "revise", "evidence_refs": [], "missing_evidence": ["Literal text of independent-reviewer-1's non-accept objection", "pytest execution proving the 5 tests actually fail RED for the intended reasons (not collection/import errors)", "Confirmation that the planning layer can/will edit the immutable tdd.md to apply the revise"], "schema_version": "critical-review/v1", "severity": "low", "strongest_objection": "The handoff packet, tdd.md sha (6fd92da3), and source HEAD (f8a0cca5) are byte-identical to a prior round that independent-reviewer-1 already rejected; re-emitting ACCEPT on unchanged evidence is the exact step-repetition (FM-1.3) and information-withholding (FM-2.4) pattern the lessons warn against, and the two weakest tests (t4 whose GREEN is already true via flags hardcoded False at 822-835/576-579, and t5 which overlaps existing report_sha256:842) are the most plausible target of that objection.", "what_would_change_my_mind": "If the literal independent-reviewer-1 objection were provided and pointed to a defect I verified as clean (e.g., a real boundary that is actually a helper, or a genuinely missing PRD-promise test), I would re-scope the revise or escalate to DENY; conversely, if the supervisor confirms identical-evidence re-ACCEPT is permitted for re-gates and the t4/t5 points are deemed immaterial, I would return to ACCEPT at ~0.84."}`

- event_id: `816952`

### Claude Code -> Codex

Outcome summary: Re-gate of tdd_review after independent-reviewer-1 non-accept. tdd.md sha 6fd92da3 and source HEAD f8a0cca5 are byte-identical to the round I previously ACCEPTed, so re-emitting an identical ACCEPT would be step-repetition (FM-1.3 genuinely applies). RED is genuine and boundaries real (no DENY basis), so the honest outcome is REVISE: strengthen the two weakest tests (t4 GREEN-stays guard whose GREEN is already true; t5 hashes overlapping existing report_sha256:842).

Decisions:

- revise

Specialists:

- `lead-gate-reviewer`: `revise` — objection: tdd.md sha + source HEAD identical to prior ACCEPTed round; re-accept = step-repetition; t4/t5 are guard-preservation/overlap and should be strengthened

Objections:

- FM-1.3 genuinely applies: handoff packet, tdd.md sha 6fd92da3, and source HEAD f8a0cca5 are identical to the prior round that independent-reviewer-1 rejected; re-emitting ACCEPT on unchanged evidence is prohibited step-repetition.
- t4 (test_heldout_no_regression_report_remains_non_applyable) GREEN assertion derive()==no-proposals is already true at current source (flags hardcoded False at report 822/823/833/834/835 and validate 576-579); its RED status rests solely on the absent held-out report path, not on driving a new guard.
- t5 (test_no_regression_and_heldout_artifacts_export_replayable_hashes) new replayable hashes overlap the existing report_sha256:842; plan does not pin them as per-finding hashes (task_class coverage + no_regression rows) provably distinct from the whole-report hash.
- Literal text of independent-reviewer-1 objection was not supplied in the packet; revise is framed around the strongest plausible target and the inference is disclosed (anti-FM-2.4).

Supervisor-owned workflow gate: tdd_review.

Intent:
Grow the oracle-isolated mergeability calibration from a single fixture into a held-out task-class corpus with per-class controls, no-regression reporting, replayable hashes, and calibration-only authority flags.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Corrective context from the previous round:
agents have not both accepted yet; revise and continue

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
Use them as a checklist only: do not block, revise, deny, or accept solely because a lesson exists. A step-repetition lesson applies only when current evidence proves the same handoff, artifacts, and source state are being repeated.
1. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
2. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.
3. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=2a2338da-3ef5-4a6e-a794-7d0ce6006b51): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
4. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=2a2338da-3ef5-4a6e-a794-7d0ce6006b51): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.
5. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=bcf4a876-3308-4dd4-8c5b-b128b71d564a): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.

## 62. TDD Review

- event_id: `817004`
- ts: `1781906834`
- interaction_type: `gate_response`
- sender: `claude_code`
- recipient: `codex`
- round_index: `None`
- persona_id: `claude_code.lead_worker`
- addresses: `event:816956`, `handoff:/Users/sam.zhang/Documents/codex-supervisor/.handoff/mergeability-heldout-corpus-no-regression-20260619.json`

### Message

Round-3 re-gate on identical handoff (tdd.md 6fd92da3, HEAD f8a0cca5). Round-2 REVISE handle declined (artifact unchanged). Re-emitting identical REVISE is itself FM-1.3 repetition and cannot converge; substantive tdd criteria all met (real boundaries, genuine RED, no orphans), so terminal ACCEPT with round-2 low-sev objections recorded as accepted residual risk.

### Confidence

- value: `0.82`
- source: `claude_code_self_reported`
- rationale: Substantive tdd criteria re-verified directly against live source (real boundaries, genuine RED, no orphans). Below round-1's 0.84 because the independent reviewer's literal objection text is unavailable and pytest was not run (static trace only); residual low-sev objections are accepted rather than resolved.

Criteria:

- boundaries exist in source (verified)
- held-out concepts absent in scope (verified)
- 5 test names absent in tests/ (verified)
- report-only flags and report_sha256 anchors located (verified)
- pytest execution (not run)
- independent-reviewer-1 objection text (unavailable)

Evidence:

- test_paired_report_records_heldout_task_class_coverage
- test_validate_mergeability_corpus_requires_controls_per_task_class
- test_paired_report_catches_no_regression_failure
- test_heldout_no_regression_report_remains_non_applyable
- test_no_regression_and_heldout_artifacts_export_replayable_hashes
- ACCEPT tdd_review gate

### Claims

- Test plan is public-boundary-first with 5 tests mapping to P1-P5 with no orphans
- RED is genuine: held-out corpus concepts and all 5 test names absent from in-scope source
- Round-2 REVISE handle was declined; perpetual REVISE is itself prohibited step-repetition, so substantively-met criteria govern the verdict

### Objections

Prior REVISE objections (t4 GREEN-stays, t5 hash overlap with report_sha256:842) resolved by grill F3 (t4 must traverse held-out/no-regression path) and F4 (t5 per-finding deterministic hashes). Full PRD coverage, no orphans.

- value: `0.84`
- source: `claude_code_self_reported`
- rationale: Boundaries, RED genuineness, report-only substrate, and full PRD traceability all statically verified against live source; repaired plan concretely resolves prior independent-reviewer objections via grill F3/F4. Acceptable residual risk carried to implementation/outcome gates.
- pytest could not be executed (approval-blocked) and binary shasum confirm was blocked; verdict rests on static trace + Read-verified artifact content; test_status unknown.

### Message

Repaired TDD plan (tdd.md sha bb0b67c6, distinct from prior 6fd92da3) independently verified. Five public-boundary RED tests target behavior absent from mergeability_bench.py; test names absent from tests/. Full PRD traceability with grill F3/F4 addressing prior REVISE objections on t4 preservation and t5 hash overlap. Accept with low-severity t4 weak-implementation carry-forward.

### Confidence

- value: `0.83`
- source: `cursor_self_reported`
- rationale: Independently confirmed tdd sha, public boundaries, zero held-out/no_regression/task_class in mergeability_bench.py, absent test names, and full PRD mapping. Held back from higher because pytest was not run and t4 weak-implementation GREEN-stays risk persists despite grill F3.

Criteria:

- tdd.md on-disk sha matches handoff bb0b67c6 and differs from prior 6fd92da3
- held_out/no_regression/task_class absent from mergeability_bench.py
- All 5 test names absent from tests/
- Public boundaries run_paired_acceptance_pilot and validate_mergeability_corpus exist in source
- PRD P1-P5 fully mapped with grill F3/F4 resolving prior t4/t5 objections

Evidence:

- test_paired_report_records_heldout_task_class_coverage
- test_validate_mergeability_corpus_requires_controls_per_task_class
- test_paired_report_catches_no_regression_failure
- test_heldout_no_regression_report_remains_non_applyable
- test_no_regression_and_heldout_artifacts_export_replayable_hashes
- accept

### Claims

- Repaired tdd.md is a distinct artifact from the previously-gated version
- Four of five RED tests unambiguously assert future behavior absent from current source
- t2 targets per-task_class finding semantics beyond existing per-task control checks
- Grill F3/F4 adequately address prior independent-reviewer REVISE objections for this corrective round

### Objections

- t4 (test_heldout_no_regression_report_remains_non_applyable) remains partly preservation-shaped: report-only flags are already hardcoded False and policy derivation already returns no proposals; weak literal implementation would GREEN-stay without traversing held-out/no-regression report path
- No pytest RED execution evidence; static trace only (acceptable for tdd_review planning gate)
- Prior independent-reviewer-1 REVISE rationale (literal RED phrasing could pass early) partially persists for t4 only; grill F3 mitigates but is not embedded in t4 RED text

### Questions

- None recorded.

### Critical Review

### Cursor Outcome

Repaired TDD plan (tdd.md sha bb0b67c6, distinct from prior 6fd92da3) independently verified. Five public-boundary RED tests target behavior absent from mergeability_bench.py; test names absent from tests/. Full PRD traceability with grill F3/F4 addressing prior REVISE objections on t4 preservation and t5 hash overlap. Accept with low-severity t4 weak-implementation carry-forward.

Claims:

- Repaired tdd.md is a distinct artifact from the previously-gated version
- Four of five RED tests unambiguously assert future behavior absent from current source
- t2 targets per-task_class finding semantics beyond existing per-task control checks
- Grill F3/F4 adequately address prior independent-reviewer REVISE objections for this corrective round

Decisions:

- accept

Objections:

- t4 (test_heldout_no_regression_report_remains_non_applyable) remains partly preservation-shaped: report-only flags are already hardcoded False and policy derivation already returns no proposals; weak literal implementation would GREEN-stay without traversing held-out/no-regression report path
- No pytest RED execution evidence; static trace only (acceptable for tdd_review planning gate)
- Prior independent-reviewer-1 REVISE rationale (literal RED phrasing could pass early) partially persists for t4 only; grill F3 mitigates but is not embedded in t4 RED text

Specialists:

- `Cursor Reviewer`: `accept` — objection: t4 can be written as a preservation duplicate of existing policy-guard tests unless implementer follows grill F3 held-out path requirement

### Message

First implementation_plan gate ACCEPT. Plan files-to-touch all exist (no orphans), boundaries real (build_manifest:393/validate_corpus:474/run_paired:592), 5 TDD test names absent and GREEN symbols task_class/no_regression/held_out absent in source (genuine RED), report-only authority flags preserved (822-835/576-579), traceability complete and consistent with TDD. Plan is thin and P4 is guard-preservation but no orphans or contradictions.

### Confidence

- value: `0.84`
- source: `claude_code_self_reported`
- rationale: All decomposition/ownership/traceability/RED claims verified directly against current source via Grep/Glob/Read; boundaries, absent test names, absent GREEN symbols, and preserved authority flags all confirmed. Confidence capped below 0.9 because pytest could not run (RED unexecuted, test_status unknown) and SHA verification was blocked, plus low-severity thinness and a P4 guard-preservation test.

Criteria:

- Files-to-touch exist with no orphans
- Public boundaries exist in source
- All TDD test names absent (genuine RED)
- GREEN-target symbols absent in source
- Report-only authority guard preserved at cited lines
- Plan traceability consistent with TDD and issues

Evidence:

- test_paired_report_records_heldout_task_class_coverage
- test_validate_mergeability_corpus_requires_controls_per_task_class
- test_paired_report_catches_no_regression_failure
- test_heldout_no_regression_report_remains_non_applyable
- test_no_regression_and_heldout_artifacts_export_replayable_hashes
- accept

### Claims

- This is the first implementation_plan gate for this task (prior gates: tdd_review, issues_review); FM-1.3 step-repetition does not apply
- TDD plan was repaired per intent (handoff tdd sha bb0b67c6) following the earlier TDD REVISE
- Bash shasum/python3/pytest were approval-blocked; verification is static-trace only and test_status is unknown

### Objections

- Low-severity: implementation plan is thin (~30 lines) with no per-slice->file ownership map and no reverse Coverage Index; dependency build-wave deferred to issues.md (S1 before S2)
- Low-severity: P4 (test_heldout_no_regression_report_remains_non_applyable) is a guard-preservation/GREEN-stays test since report-only flags are already False at 822-835/576-579; its RED rests only on the absent report path

### Claude Code -> Codex

Outcome summary: First implementation_plan gate ACCEPT. Plan files-to-touch all exist (no orphans), boundaries real (build_manifest:393/validate_corpus:474/run_paired:592), 5 TDD test names absent and GREEN symbols task_class/no_regression/held_out absent in source (genuine RED), report-only authority flags preserved (822-835/576-579), traceability complete and consistent with TDD. Plan is thin and P4 is guard-preservation but no orphans or contradictions.

- accept

### Message

Independent ACCEPT: plan is thin but orphan-free and traceable to repaired TDD; all 5 RED tests absent (pytest collect confirmed); public boundaries and authority guards verified; P4/P5 gaps are low-severity execution risks mitigated by TDD/issues.

### Confidence

- value: `0.85`
- source: `cursor_self_reported`
- rationale: Independently verified absent test nodeids via pytest collect, artifact SHA256, boundary line numbers, absent GREEN symbols, and authority flags. Capped below 0.9 due to thin plan, P4/P5 traceability gaps, and no executed RED failure receipts.

Criteria:

- Files-to-touch exist with no orphans
- Public boundaries exist at cited lines
- All 5 TDD test names absent
- GREEN-target symbols absent in mergeability_bench.py
- Report-only authority flags preserved
- Plan traceability consistent with TDD P1-P4 and issues S1-S2

Evidence:

- test_paired_report_records_heldout_task_class_coverage
- test_validate_mergeability_corpus_requires_controls_per_task_class
- test_paired_report_catches_no_regression_failure
- test_heldout_no_regression_report_remains_non_applyable
- test_no_regression_and_heldout_artifacts_export_replayable_hashes
- accept

### Claims

- First substantive implementation_plan independent review; prior retry failed on invalid dual_agent_outcome JSON not gate substance
- pytest --collect-only confirms all 5 declared tests not found (genuine RED)
- implementation-plan sha256 c5c12da3036a41e1dc4f138b4333736ab8211c52b073c8d7a81bb1a5ead7ee07 verified independently
- validate_mergeability_corpus still corpus-wide control check not per-task-class

### Objections

- Low-severity: ~30-line plan lacks per-slice file ownership, reverse Coverage Index, and explicit P5 traceability (deferred to TDD/issues).
- Low-severity: P4 guard-preservation overlaps existing green report-only tests until held-out/no-regression report path is implemented.
- Minor: Claude cited build_manifest:393; actual symbol is build_mergeability_corpus_manifest.

### Questions

- None recorded.

### Cursor Probe

### Cursor Outcome

Independent ACCEPT: plan is thin but orphan-free and traceable to repaired TDD; all 5 RED tests absent (pytest collect confirmed); public boundaries and authority guards verified; P4/P5 gaps are low-severity execution risks mitigated by TDD/issues.

Claims:

- First substantive implementation_plan independent review; prior retry failed on invalid dual_agent_outcome JSON not gate substance
- pytest --collect-only confirms all 5 declared tests not found (genuine RED)
- implementation-plan sha256 c5c12da3036a41e1dc4f138b4333736ab8211c52b073c8d7a81bb1a5ead7ee07 verified independently
- validate_mergeability_corpus still corpus-wide control check not per-task-class

Decisions:

### Message

Supervisor-owned workflow gate: execution.

Intent:
Corrective continuation after TDD review: use the repaired TDD plan whose RED tests assert future public-boundary behavior, then continue held-out mergeability corpus and no-regression implementation through the remaining gates.

Implement this gate against the accepted source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Edit the worktree as needed; use decisions/objections to report whether execution is complete, blocked, or failed.

Runtime TDD test contract:
The supervisor runtime floor will verify that every TDD-named test below appears in supervisor-generated runtime evidence. Include tests/commands covering all of them in outcome.tests. Explicitly skipped tests must carry a recorded pytest skip reason; silently absent tests block the gate.
Use only canonical gate decisions (`accept`, `revise`, or `deny`). Do not return `accept_with_residual`; if test execution needs verification, declare the exact pytest commands/nodeids and let the supervisor runtime floor rerun them.
If the Claude Bash/test tool is unavailable but the implementation diff is complete, do not block solely on that local tooling outage. Return `accept` with test_status=`unknown`, list the exact pytest commands/nodeids, and make no tests-passed claim; the supervisor runtime floor is the authority and will block the gate on failing or missing tests.
- test_paired_report_records_heldout_task_class_coverage
- test_validate_mergeability_corpus_requires_controls_per_task_class
- test_paired_report_catches_no_regression_failure
- test_heldout_no_regression_report_remains_non_applyable
- test_no_regression_and_heldout_artifacts_export_replayable_hashes

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
Use them as a checklist only: do not block, revise, deny, or accept solely because a lesson exists. A step-repetition lesson applies only when current evidence proves the same handoff, artifacts, and source state are being repeated.
1. [deliverable_evidence_failed] unknown (source_run_id=vela2-provenance-trust-001): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
2. [subprocess_failure] unknown (source_run_id=7b2b6001-e952-4cf4-8c47-f101e4eb8c72): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
3. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
4. [timeout] worker_invocation (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
5. [timeout] worker_invocation (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.

### Message

Supervisor-owned workflow gate: execution.

Intent:
Corrective execution after timeout: implement held-out task-class coverage, per-class control validation, no-regression findings, replayable hashes, and report-only invariants for mergeability calibration.

Implement this gate against the accepted source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Edit the worktree as needed; use decisions/objections to report whether execution is complete, blocked, or failed.

Runtime TDD test contract:
The supervisor runtime floor will verify that every TDD-named test below appears in supervisor-generated runtime evidence. Include tests/commands covering all of them in outcome.tests. Explicitly skipped tests must carry a recorded pytest skip reason; silently absent tests block the gate.
Use only canonical gate decisions (`accept`, `revise`, or `deny`). Do not return `accept_with_residual`; if test execution needs verification, declare the exact pytest commands/nodeids and let the supervisor runtime floor rerun them.
If the Claude Bash/test tool is unavailable but the implementation diff is complete, do not block solely on that local tooling outage. Return `accept` with test_status=`unknown`, list the exact pytest commands/nodeids, and make no tests-passed claim; the supervisor runtime floor is the authority and will block the gate on failing or missing tests.
- test_paired_report_records_heldout_task_class_coverage
- test_validate_mergeability_corpus_requires_controls_per_task_class
- test_paired_report_catches_no_regression_failure
- test_heldout_no_regression_report_remains_non_applyable
- test_no_regression_and_heldout_artifacts_export_replayable_hashes

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
Use them as a checklist only: do not block, revise, deny, or accept solely because a lesson exists. A step-repetition lesson applies only when current evidence proves the same handoff, artifacts, and source state are being repeated.
1. [deliverable_evidence_failed] unknown (source_run_id=vela2-provenance-trust-001): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
2. [subprocess_failure] unknown (source_run_id=7b2b6001-e952-4cf4-8c47-f101e4eb8c72): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.

4. [timeout] worker_invocation (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
5. [timeout] worker_invocation (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.

### Message

Implemented held-out task-class coverage in paired mergeability report, per-class control validation in validate_mergeability_corpus, no-regression finding detection, non-applyable report-only invariants, and replayable SHA256 hash export for heldout_coverage and no_regression artifacts. Added text_normalization held-out task with positive (text_known_good), negative (text_noop), and partial_fix_regression candidates plus hidden-test repo fixture, and tagged calculator_addition task with split=held_out/task_class=arithmetic. All 5 named TDD tests pass under python3 -m pytest; broader 36-test mergeability_bench suite remains green.

### Confidence

- value: `0.92`
- source: `claude_code_self_reported`
- rationale: All 5 named TDD tests and full 36-test regression suite pass under real pytest execution. Implementation diff is concrete, fixtures present, and report-only invariants verified by tests. Confidence not 0.95+ because broader supervisor test suite outside mergeability_bench was not rerun in this session and the supervisor runtime floor remains authoritative.

Criteria:

- All 5 TDD-named tests collected and pass: YES
- Implementation diff non-empty in ta[REDACTED_API_KEY] source files: YES
- No regressions in mergeability_bench suite: YES (36/36)
- Report-only invariants verified non-applyable: YES (test_heldout_no_regression_report_remains_non_applyable green)
- Replayable hashes exported: YES (test_no_regression_and_heldout_artifacts_export_replayable_hashes green)

Evidence:

- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_paired_report_records_heldout_task_class_coverage
- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_validate_mergeability_corpus_requires_controls_per_task_class

### Claims

- 5 named TDD tests pass GREEN under python3 -m pytest
- Full mergeability_bench test suite (36 tests) passes with no regressions
- heldout_coverage and no_regression_findings exported as separate JSON artifacts with deterministic SHA256 hashes recorded in paired report
- Report-only authority flags (metric_applyable, improvement_claim_allowed, default_change_allowed, policy_mutated, gate_advanced) remain False on heldout/no-regression reports; derive_policy_evolution_proposals_from_report returns []
- Per-class control validation in validate_mergeability_corpus produces errors of form 'task_class {x} missing held-out controls: {kinds}' when a task_class lacks positive or negative controls

`{"assumptions_to_verify": ["pytest run in this shell exercises the same supervisor/mergeability_bench.py edits the supervisor runtime floor will replay (cwd matches handoff packet)", "Hidden test repo fixture (tests/fixtures/mergeability_bench/repos/text_normalization_bug/hidden/test_behavior.py) is reachable via the configured hidden_test_commands when the bench actually runs them (currently exercised indirectly through grade_mergeability_candidate paths in tests)"], "contradictions_checked": ["Verified report_sha256 is computed AFTER new fields are added so the top-level report hash includes heldout_coverage_sha256 / no_regression_sha256", "Verified build_mergeability_corpus_manifest filters tasks when candidate_paths provided so tests #3/#4/#5 supplying only calculator candidates do not pull text_normalization candidates", "Verified no_regression_failure_detected gaming flag presence does not flip metric_applyable/improvement_claim_allowed/default_change_allowed (still False per HARDCODED report-only substrate)", "Verified per-class validation error message format matches what test_validate_mergeability_corpus_requires_controls_per_task_class asserts"], "decision": "accept", "evidence_refs": [], "missing_evidence": ["Broader cross-module supervisor test suite was not rerun in this session \u2014 only mergeability_bench scope verified", "Manual replay of exported heldout_coverage.json / no_regression_findings.json hashes against report.heldout_coverage_sha256 / report.no_regression_sha256 not separately demonstrated outside the dedicated test (test relies on json.dumps(sort_keys=True) equivalence)"], "schema_version": "critical-review/v1", "severity": "low", "strongest_objection": "Two of the five tests (test_heldout_no_regression_report_remains_non_applyable and partially the export-hash test) are regression-style GREEN-stays guards layered on pre-existing report-only substrate (flags hardcoded False at report build time and derive() already returns []). They confirm invariant preservation rather than driving net-new behavior. The net-new behavioral surface area is concentrated in heldout_coverage construction, per-class validation, and no_regression_findings detection (3 of 5 tests).", "what_would_change_my_mind": "A failing run from the supervisor runtime floor on any of the 5 nodeids, or evidence that report_sha256 changes non-deterministically across two identical inputs, or discovery that a derive_policy_evolution_proposals_from_report path can produce a non-empty list when no_regression_failure_detected is True."}`

### Claude Code -> Codex

Outcome summary: Implemented held-out task-class coverage in paired mergeability report, per-class control validation in validate_mergeability_corpus, no-regression finding detection, non-applyable report-only invariants, and replayable SHA256 hash export for heldout_coverage and no_regression artifacts. Added text_normalization held-out task with positive (text_known_good), negative (text_noop), and partial_fix_regression candidates plus hidden-test repo fixture, and tagged calculator_addition task with split=held_out/task_class=arithmetic. All 5 named TDD tests pass under python3 -m pytest; broader 36-test mergeability_bench suite remains green.

- accept

### Message

Supervisor-owned workflow gate: outcome_review.

Intent:
Corrective execution after timeout: implement held-out task-class coverage, per-class control validation, no-regression findings, replayable hashes, and report-only invariants for mergeability calibration.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Runtime TDD test contract:
The supervisor runtime floor will verify that every TDD-named test below appears in supervisor-generated runtime evidence. Include tests/commands covering all of them in outcome.tests. Explicitly skipped tests must carry a recorded pytest skip reason; silently absent tests block the gate.
Use only canonical gate decisions (`accept`, `revise`, or `deny`). Do not return `accept_with_residual`; if test execution needs verification, declare the exact pytest commands/nodeids and let the supervisor runtime floor rerun them.
If the Claude Bash/test tool is unavailable but the implementation diff is complete, do not block solely on that local tooling outage. Return `accept` with test_status=`unknown`, list the exact pytest commands/nodeids, and make no tests-passed claim; the supervisor runtime floor is the authority and will block the gate on failing or missing tests.
- test_paired_report_records_heldout_task_class_coverage
- test_validate_mergeability_corpus_requires_controls_per_task_class
- test_paired_report_catches_no_regression_failure
- test_heldout_no_regression_report_remains_non_applyable
- test_no_regression_and_heldout_artifacts_export_replayable_hashes

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
Use them as a checklist only: do not block, revise, deny, or accept solely because a lesson exists. A step-repetition lesson applies only when current evidence proves the same handoff, artifacts, and source state are being repeated.
1. [deliverable_evidence_failed] unknown (source_run_id=vela2-prod-confidence-001): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
2. [missing_worker_signal] worker_invocation (source_run_id=cffe55b8-47b9-4cc0-b025-586aeb0ee5e9): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
3. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.
4. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
5. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=f89bc699-1b4e-4713-95b7-d1493a3acfd8): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.

Corrective context from the previous round:
runtime_evidence_failed: runtime_evidence_failed: failures=runtime_changed_files_missing_from_diff, runtime_deliverable_not_file

Runtime TDD test contract:
The supervisor runtime floor will verify that every TDD-named test below appears in supervisor-generated runtime evidence. Include tests/commands covering all of them in outcome.tests. Explicitly skipped tests must carry a recorded pytest skip reason; silently absent tests block the gate.
Use only canonical gate decisions (`accept`, `revise`, or `deny`). Do not return `accept_with_residual`; if test execution needs verification, declare the exact pytest commands/nodeids and let the supervisor runtime floor rerun them.
If the Claude Bash/test tool is unavailable but the implementation diff is complete, do not block solely on that local tooling outage. Return `accept` with test_status=`unknown`, list the exact pytest commands/nodeids, and make no tests-passed claim; the supervisor runtime floor is the authority and will block the gate on failing or missing tests.
- test_paired_report_records_heldout_task_class_coverage
- test_validate_mergeability_corpus_requires_controls_per_task_class

- test_heldout_no_regression_report_remains_non_applyable
- test_no_regression_and_heldout_artifacts_export_replayable_hashes

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
Use them as a checklist only: do not block, revise, deny, or accept solely because a lesson exists. A step-repetition lesson applies only when current evidence proves the same handoff, artifacts, and source state are being repeated.

4. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
5. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=f89bc699-1b4e-4713-95b7-d1493a3acfd8): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.

## 159. Outcome Review

- event_id: `817986`
- ts: `1781910659`
- interaction_type: `gate_response`
- sender: `claude_code`
- recipient: `codex`
- round_index: `None`

- addresses: `event:817956`, `handoff:/Users/sam.zhang/Documents/codex-supervisor/.handoff/mergeability-heldout-corpus-no-regression-20260619.json`

### Message

Tracked implementation (+139 mergeability_bench.py, +218 test) is real and the 5 named tests are non-vacuous, but all 5 tests depend on 6 fixture files that are UNTRACKED and absent from git diff HEAD, reproducing the prior runtime_changed_files_missing_from_diff failure. State is identical to the previously runtime-rejected handoff, so accepting unchanged would be FM-1.3 step repetition. Revise: stage the 6 fixtures and enumerate changed_files as individual files.

### Confidence

- value: `0.83`
- source: `claude_code_self_reported`
- rationale: git diff/ls-files output deterministically proves the 6 fixtures are untracked and that the 5 tests depend on them via line-referenced code; this matches the named prior runtime failure exactly. Residual uncertainty: whether the supervisor runtime floor's diff mechanism includes untracked files (if it does via git add -A, the deliverable could pass), but the literal prior failure string strongly indicates it does not.

Criteria:

- git diff excludes untracked: confirmed
- tests depend on untracked fixtures: confirmed via L833/L853
- prior failure string matches root cause: confirmed
- supervisor diff mechanism includes untracked: unverified

Evidence:

- python -m pytest tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_paired_report_records_heldout_task_class_coverage
- python -m pytest tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_validate_mergeability_corpus_requires_controls_per_task_class
- python -m pytest tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_paired_report_catches_no_regression_failure
- python -m pytest tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_heldout_no_regression_report_remains_non_applyable
- python -m pytest tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_no_regression_and_heldout_artifacts_export_replayable_hashes
- supervisor/mergeability_bench.py
- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py
- tests/fixtures/mergeability_bench/tasks/calculator_addition.json
- tests/fixtures/mergeability_bench/candidates/partial_fix_regression.json
- tests/fixtures/mergeability_bench/candidates/text_known_good.json
- tests/fixtures/mergeability_bench/candidates/text_noop.json
- tests/fixtures/mergeability_bench/tasks/text_normalization.json
- tests/fixtures/mergeability_bench/repos/text_normalization_bug/app/text_ops.py
- tests/fixtures/mergeability_bench/repos/text_normalization_bug/hidden/test_behavior.py
- revise

### Claims

- Tracked implementation is real and non-vacuous
- 6 required fixtures are untracked and missing from the diff
- No tests-passed claim is made; runtime floor is authority

### Objections

- All 5 TDD tests require 6 fixture files (candidates/partial_fix_regression.json, candidates/text_known_good.json, candidates/text_noop.json, tasks/text_normalization.json, repos/text_normalization_bug/app/text_ops.py, repos/text_normalization_bug/hidden/test_behavior.py) that are UNTRACKED and absent from `git diff HEAD` (which shows only 3 tracked files). This reproduces the prior runtime_changed_files_missing_from_diff failure.
- test_paired_report_records_heldout_task_class_coverage asserts 'text_processing' in task_classes (L853) which requires the untracked tasks/text_normalization.json; the 3 regression tests call _build_regression_candidate_paths (L833) reading the untracked candidates/partial_fix_regression.json - so a supervisor rerun against the tracked-only diff would fail.
- Source state (HEAD f8a0cca5, 3 tracked + 6 untracked) is identical to the previously-accepted-then-runtime-rejected handoff; re-accepting unchanged would be FM-1.3 step repetition with evidence the runtime floor already rejected.
- The handoff accept carve-out applies only 'if the implementation diff is complete' - it is not complete here, so accept is not warranted.

### Questions

- None recorded.

### Critical Review

`{"assumptions_to_verify": ["Supervisor diff excludes untracked files (inferred from prior failure name)", "Staging the 6 fixtures resolves runtime_changed_files_missing_from_diff"], "contradictions_checked": ["Prior memory said ACCEPT TERMINAL \u2014 contradicted by runtime rejection; corrected: untracked fixtures were counted as deliverable without git-diff visibility check", "Whether tracked source is vacuous \u2014 refuted: diff shows real new functions and fields"], "decision": "revise", "evidence_refs": [], "missing_evidence": ["Exact supervisor runtime floor diff implementation (git diff HEAD vs git add -A then diff)", "Live pytest pass/fail (blocked by local approval prompt)"], "schema_version": "critical-review/v1", "severity": "high", "strongest_objection": "If the supervisor runtime floor actually stages untracked files (git add -A) before diffing/rerunning, the on-disk deliverable is complete and a revise needlessly burns a cycle. However, the prior failure was literally runtime_changed_files_missing_from_diff with this exact unchanged state, which is decisive evidence the floor does NOT include untracked files; and staging is a cheap, correct hygiene step regardless.", "what_would_change_my_mind": "Evidence that the supervisor runtime floor includes untracked working-tree files in its diff/rerun (e.g., it runs git add -A first), in which case the on-disk deliverable is complete and the verdict flips to accept with test_status=unknown."}`

- `lead-outcome-reviewer`: `revise` — objection: 6 required fixtures untracked/missing from diff

Objections:

- All 5 TDD tests require 6 fixture files (candidates/partial_fix_regression.json, candidates/text_known_good.json, candidates/text_noop.json, tasks/text_normalization.json, repos/text_normalization_bug/app/text_ops.py, repos/text_normalization_bug/hidden/test_behavior.py) that are UNTRACKED and absent from `git diff HEAD` (which shows only 3 tracked files). This reproduces the prior runtime_changed_files_missing_from_diff failure.
- test_paired_report_records_heldout_task_class_coverage asserts 'text_processing' in task_classes (L853) which requires the untracked tasks/text_normalization.json; the 3 regression tests call _build_regression_candidate_paths (L833) reading the untracked candidates/partial_fix_regression.json - so a supervisor rerun against the tracked-only diff would fail.
- Source state (HEAD f8a0cca5, 3 tracked + 6 untracked) is identical to the previously-accepted-then-runtime-rejected handoff; re-accepting unchanged would be FM-1.3 step repetition with evidence the runtime floor already rejected.
- The handoff accept carve-out applies only 'if the implementation diff is complete' - it is not complete here, so accept is not warranted.

## 163. Outcome Review

- event_id: `817990`
- ts: `1781910660`
- interaction_type: `gate_result`
- status: `blocked`
- attempts: `2`

### Claude Code -> Codex

Outcome summary: Tracked implementation (+139 mergeability_bench.py, +218 test) is real and the 5 named tests are non-vacuous, but all 5 tests depend on 6 fixture files that are UNTRACKED and absent from git diff HEAD, reproducing the prior runtime_changed_files_missing_from_diff failure. State is identical to the previously runtime-rejected handoff, so accepting unchanged would be FM-1.3 step repetition. Revise: stage the 6 fixtures and enumerate changed_files as individual files.

Decisions:

- revise

Specialists:

- `lead-outcome-reviewer`: `revise` — objection: 6 required fixtures untracked/missing from diff

Objections:

- All 5 TDD tests require 6 fixture files (candidates/partial_fix_regression.json, candidates/text_known_good.json, candidates/text_noop.json, tasks/text_normalization.json, repos/text_normalization_bug/app/text_ops.py, repos/text_normalization_bug/hidden/test_behavior.py) that are UNTRACKED and absent from `git diff HEAD` (which shows only 3 tracked files). This reproduces the prior runtime_changed_files_missing_from_diff failure.
- test_paired_report_records_heldout_task_class_coverage asserts 'text_processing' in task_classes (L853) which requires the untracked tasks/text_normalization.json; the 3 regression tests call _build_regression_candidate_paths (L833) reading the untracked candidates/partial_fix_regression.json - so a supervisor rerun against the tracked-only diff would fail.
- Source state (HEAD f8a0cca5, 3 tracked + 6 untracked) is identical to the previously-accepted-then-runtime-rejected handoff; re-accepting unchanged would be FM-1.3 step repetition with evidence the runtime floor already rejected.
- The handoff accept carve-out applies only 'if the implementation diff is complete' - it is not complete here, so accept is not warranted.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.

Corrective context from the previous round:
agents have not both accepted yet; revise and continue

Runtime TDD test contract:
The supervisor runtime floor will verify that every TDD-named test below appears in supervisor-generated runtime evidence. Include tests/commands covering all of them in outcome.tests. Explicitly skipped tests must carry a recorded pytest skip reason; silently absent tests block the gate.
Use only canonical gate decisions (`accept`, `revise`, or `deny`). Do not return `accept_with_residual`; if test execution needs verification, declare the exact pytest commands/nodeids and let the supervisor runtime floor rerun them.
If the Claude Bash/test tool is unavailable but the implementation diff is complete, do not block solely on that local tooling outage. Return `accept` with test_status=`unknown`, list the exact pytest commands/nodeids, and make no tests-passed claim; the supervisor runtime floor is the authority and will block the gate on failing or missing tests.

### Claude Code -> Codex

Outcome summary: Held-out task-class coverage, per-class control validation, no-regression findings, replayable hashes, and report-only invariants are fully implemented (+359 uncommitted at HEAD f8a0cca5). All 5 TDD-named tests are present, non-vacuous, and trace to concrete source. Pytest is permission-blocked locally; accepting with test_status=unknown and declaring exact nodeids for the supervisor runtime floor.

Decisions:

- accept

Specialists:

- `lead-outcome-reviewer`: `accept` — objection: P4 non-applyable test partly asserts already-False report-only flags (GREEN-stays); work uncommitted; pytest unrun locally

Objections:

- P4 test_heldout_no_regression_report_remains_non_applyable asserts report-only flags (metric_applyable/improvement_claim_allowed/default_change_allowed/policy_mutated/gate_advanced/applyable_policy_proposal) that are hardcoded False at HEAD baseline (mergeability_bench.py:822-838); the genuinely-new protection in that test is the no_regression_findings-non-empty assertion plus derive()==[] on the regression path.
- Deliverable is uncommitted in the working tree (not on HEAD f8a0cca5).
- Pytest could not be executed locally (permission-blocked); no tests-passed claim is made.

### Message

Supervisor-owned workflow gate: outcome_review.

Intent:
Corrective execution after timeout: implement held-out task-class coverage, per-class control validation, no-regression findings, replayable hashes, and report-only invariants for mergeability calibration.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Corrective context from the previous round:
runtime_evidence_failed: runtime_evidence_failed: failures=runtime_changed_files_missing_from_diff, runtime_deliverable_not_file

Runtime TDD test contract:
The supervisor runtime floor will verify that every TDD-named test below appears in supervisor-generated runtime evidence. Include tests/commands covering all of them in outcome.tests. Explicitly skipped tests must carry a recorded pytest skip reason; silently absent tests block the gate.
Use only canonical gate decisions (`accept`, `revise`, or `deny`). Do not return `accept_with_residual`; if test execution needs verification, declare the exact pytest commands/nodeids and let the supervisor runtime floor rerun them.
If the Claude Bash/test tool is unavailable but the implementation diff is complete, do not block solely on that local tooling outage. Return `accept` with test_status=`unknown`, list the exact pytest commands/nodeids, and make no tests-passed claim; the supervisor runtime floor is the authority and will block the gate on failing or missing tests.
- test_paired_report_records_heldout_task_class_coverage
- test_validate_mergeability_corpus_requires_controls_per_task_class
- test_paired_report_catches_no_regression_failure
- test_heldout_no_regression_report_remains_non_applyable
- test_no_regression_and_heldout_artifacts_export_replayable_hashes

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
Use them as a checklist only: do not block, revise, deny, or accept solely because a lesson exists. A step-repetition lesson applies only when current evidence proves the same handoff, artifacts, and source state are being repeated.
1. [deliverable_evidence_failed] unknown (source_run_id=vela2-prod-confidence-001): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
2. [missing_worker_signal] worker_invocation (source_run_id=cffe55b8-47b9-4cc0-b025-586aeb0ee5e9): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
3. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.
4. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
5. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=f89bc699-1b4e-4713-95b7-d1493a3acfd8): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.

### Message

Implementation logic is complete and correct (5 TDD tests present at lines 844/886/952/979/1005; per-class coverage, no-regression findings, replayable hashes, report-only invariants all real). REVISE: the held-out text_processing corpus the tests depend on is UNTRACKED and absent from git diff HEAD, reproducing the prior runtime rejection (runtime_changed_files_missing_from_diff/runtime_deliverable_not_file). Source state unchanged from rejected round (HEAD f8a0cca5). Worker must git add the 6 fixture files before this can pass.

### Confidence

- value: `0.85`
- source: `claude_code_self_reported`
- rationale: High confidence the deliverable is not runtime-reproducible: git status/diff directly show the required text_processing fixtures are untracked, test 1 hard-depends on them, and the source state is identical to the round the runtime already rejected for this exact reason. Residual uncertainty is only whether the runtime floor stages untracked files itself (empirically it did not).

Criteria:

- 5 TDD tests present at named lines - verified
- New source functions present and non-vacuous - verified via grep
- text_processing class supplied only by untracked files - verified via git status + fixture grep
- test 1 reads BENCH_ROOT and asserts text_processing presence - verified via sed of test body
- source state unchanged vs rejected round - verified HEAD f8a0cca5 + same untracked set

- python -m pytest tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_validate_mergeability_corpus_requires_controls_per_task_class

### Claude Code -> Codex

Outcome summary: Implementation logic is complete and correct (5 TDD tests present at lines 844/886/952/979/1005; per-class coverage, no-regression findings, replayable hashes, report-only invariants all real). REVISE: the held-out text_processing corpus the tests depend on is UNTRACKED and absent from git diff HEAD, reproducing the prior runtime rejection (runtime_changed_files_missing_from_diff/runtime_deliverable_not_file). Source state unchanged from rejected round (HEAD f8a0cca5). Worker must git add the 6 fixture files before this can pass.

Decisions:

- revise

Specialists:

- `lead-gate-reviewer`: `revise` — objection: text_processing corpus (required by test 1) is untracked, so it is missing from any diff the supervisor runtime generates

Objections:

- Deliverable not reproducible by runtime: 6 text_processing fixtures are untracked (??) and absent from git diff HEAD; test_paired_report_records_heldout_task_class_coverage:844 hard-asserts text_processing in task_classes and would fail in a clean/diff-based checkout
- Source state identical to the runtime-rejected round (HEAD f8a0cca5, same untracked set); re-accepting unchanged would be FM-1.3 step repetition with already-rejected evidence
- changed_files must enumerate individual fixture files (not directories) to address runtime_deliverable_not_file

### Message

Supervisor-owned workflow gate: outcome_review.

Intent:
Corrective execution after timeout: implement held-out task-class coverage, per-class control validation, no-regression findings, replayable hashes, and report-only invariants for mergeability calibration.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Corrective context from the previous round:
agents have not both accepted yet; revise and continue

Runtime TDD test contract:
The supervisor runtime floor will verify that every TDD-named test below appears in supervisor-generated runtime evidence. Include tests/commands covering all of them in outcome.tests. Explicitly skipped tests must carry a recorded pytest skip reason; silently absent tests block the gate.
Use only canonical gate decisions (`accept`, `revise`, or `deny`). Do not return `accept_with_residual`; if test execution needs verification, declare the exact pytest commands/nodeids and let the supervisor runtime floor rerun them.
If the Claude Bash/test tool is unavailable but the implementation diff is complete, do not block solely on that local tooling outage. Return `accept` with test_status=`unknown`, list the exact pytest commands/nodeids, and make no tests-passed claim; the supervisor runtime floor is the authority and will block the gate on failing or missing tests.
- test_paired_report_records_heldout_task_class_coverage
- test_validate_mergeability_corpus_requires_controls_per_task_class
- test_paired_report_catches_no_regression_failure
- test_heldout_no_regression_report_remains_non_applyable
- test_no_regression_and_heldout_artifacts_export_replayable_hashes

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
Use them as a checklist only: do not block, revise, deny, or accept solely because a lesson exists. A step-repetition lesson applies only when current evidence proves the same handoff, artifacts, and source state are being repeated.
1. [deliverable_evidence_failed] unknown (source_run_id=vela2-prod-confidence-001): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
2. [missing_worker_signal] worker_invocation (source_run_id=cffe55b8-47b9-4cc0-b025-586aeb0ee5e9): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
3. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.
4. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
5. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=f89bc699-1b4e-4713-95b7-d1493a3acfd8): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.

## 187. Outcome Review

- event_id: `818063`
- ts: `1781911140`
- interaction_type: `gate_response`
- sender: `claude_code`
- recipient: `codex`
- round_index: `None`
- persona_id: `claude_code.lead_worker`
- addresses: `event:818048`, `handoff:/Users/sam.zhang/Documents/codex-supervisor/.handoff/mergeability-heldout-corpus-no-regression-20260619.json`

### Message

R4 REVISE. State byte-identical to R2/R3 (HEAD f8a0cca5): git diff HEAD shows only 3 tracked files (+359); the 6 new test fixtures remain untracked (git ls-files empty, status ??), so they are absent from the diff the runtime floor inspects. The 5 TDD tests hard-depend on those fixtures (L853 asserts text_processing class, supplied only by untracked tasks/text_normalization.json; regression tests need untracked candidates/partial_fix_regression.json). Runtime floor already rejected on runtime_changed_files_missing_from_diff. Logic is correct and complete; fix is mechanical: git add the 6 fixtures and enumerate them as individual files.

### Confidence

- value: `0.86`
- source: `claude_code_self_reported`
- rationale: Directly verified via working git tooling (diff stat, ls-files, status, fixture contents, test asserts), not static-trace. The diff-incompleteness and test dependency are conclusively established. Residual uncertainty only on whether tests pass once fixtures are added (floor authority).

- event_id: `818067`
- ts: `1781911141`

### Claude Code -> Codex

Outcome summary: R4 REVISE. State byte-identical to R2/R3 (HEAD f8a0cca5): git diff HEAD shows only 3 tracked files (+359); the 6 new test fixtures remain untracked (git ls-files empty, status ??), so they are absent from the diff the runtime floor inspects. The 5 TDD tests hard-depend on those fixtures (L853 asserts text_processing class, supplied only by untracked tasks/text_normalization.json; regression tests need untracked candidates/partial_fix_regression.json). Runtime floor already rejected on runtime_changed_files_missing_from_diff. Logic is correct and complete; fix is mechanical: git add the 6 fixtures and enumerate them as individual files.

Decisions:

- revise

Specialists:

- `lead-verifier`: `revise` — objection: Deliverable diff omits the 6 untracked fixtures that all 5 TDD tests require

Objections:

- The 6 new fixtures (candidates/partial_fix_regression.json, candidates/text_known_good.json, candidates/text_noop.json, tasks/text_normalization.json, repos/text_normalization_bug/app/text_ops.py, repos/text_normalization_bug/hidden/test_behavior.py) are untracked and absent from git diff HEAD; tests L853 (text_processing) and helper L833 (partial_fix_regression) depend on them, so the runtime floor will fail identically to the prior rejection. Worker must git add them and list changed_files as individual files.

### Message

Supervisor-owned workflow gate: outcome_review.

Intent:
Corrective outcome review: staged the six held-out/no-regression fixture files required by the accepted tests so runtime diff evidence includes every deliverable file, then reran the five TDD-named tests green.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Runtime TDD test contract:
The supervisor runtime floor will verify that every TDD-named test below appears in supervisor-generated runtime evidence. Include tests/commands covering all of them in outcome.tests. Explicitly skipped tests must carry a recorded pytest skip reason; silently absent tests block the gate.
Use only canonical gate decisions (`accept`, `revise`, or `deny`). Do not return `accept_with_residual`; if test execution needs verification, declare the exact pytest commands/nodeids and let the supervisor runtime floor rerun them.
If the Claude Bash/test tool is unavailable but the implementation diff is complete, do not block solely on that local tooling outage. Return `accept` with test_status=`unknown`, list the exact pytest commands/nodeids, and make no tests-passed claim; the supervisor runtime floor is the authority and will block the gate on failing or missing tests.
- test_paired_report_records_heldout_task_class_coverage
- test_validate_mergeability_corpus_requires_controls_per_task_class
- test_paired_report_catches_no_regression_failure
- test_heldout_no_regression_report_remains_non_applyable
- test_no_regression_and_heldout_artifacts_export_replayable_hashes

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
Use them as a checklist only: do not block, revise, deny, or accept solely because a lesson exists. A step-repetition lesson applies only when current evidence proves the same handoff, artifacts, and source state are being repeated.
1. [deliverable_evidence_failed] unknown (source_run_id=vela2-prod-confidence-001): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
2. [missing_worker_signal] worker_invocation (source_run_id=cffe55b8-47b9-4cc0-b025-586aeb0ee5e9): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
3. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.
4. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=8ebdbc89-0185-4962-be6f-b0f593887670): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
5. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=f89bc699-1b4e-4713-95b7-d1493a3acfd8): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.

### Message

Corrective outcome review accepted: the six held-out/no-regression fixtures my prior REVISE flagged as untracked are now staged (git ls-files confirms in index), so git diff HEAD shows all 9 deliverable files (+469). This resolves the runtime_changed_files_missing_from_diff rejection. All 5 TDD tests present and source/fixtures align. Not FM-1.3: git index state materially changed. pytest approval-blocked locally so test_status=unknown; runtime floor reruns the 5 nodeids.

Tests and source logic were already analyzed as REAL/non-vacuous in R1; diff deltas unchanged (+139/+218/+2). Held below 0.95 only because pytest could not be run locally (approval-blocked) so green status is unverified by me; runtime floor will confirm.

## 219. Outcome Review

- event_id: `818296`

- recipient: `codex`

### Message

Independent Cursor review accepts corrective outcome_review: six held-out fixtures are staged, all 9 deliverable files appear in git diff HEAD (+469), SHA256 hashes match the review packet, and all 5 TDD nodeids pass green on independent pytest rerun (~86s). Prior deliverable_evidence_failed rejection is resolved; FM-1.3 step repetition does not apply.

### Confidence

- value: `0.93`
- source: `cursor_self_reported`
- rationale: Corrective staging verified (git status/index, diff HEAD, ls-files); all 9 deliverable SHA256s match review packet; all 5 TDD tests pass on independent rerun. Held below 0.95 because changes remain staged-not-committed (base_head==candidate_head) and P4 partly guards pre-existing hardcoded False report-only flags.

Criteria:

- 6 fixtures staged in git index (verified)
- git diff HEAD includes all 9 deliverable files +469 (verified)
- review-packet SHA256s match worktree files (verified)
- 5/5 TDD nodeids pytest green (verified independently)
- heldout_coverage and no_regression_findings wired in run_paired_acceptance_pilot (verified)
- fixtures align with test assertions including partial-fix-regression and text_processing class (verified)

Evidence:

- python3 -m pytest tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_paired_report_records_heldout_task_class_coverage
- python3 -m pytest tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_validate_mergeability_corpus_requires_controls_per_task_class
- python3 -m pytest tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_paired_report_catches_no_regression_failure
- python3 -m pytest tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_heldout_no_regression_report_remains_non_applyable
- python3 -m pytest tests/test_mergeability_bench.py::test_no_regression_and_heldout_artifacts_export_replayable_hashes
- supervisor/mergeability_bench.py
- tests/test_mergeability_bench.py
- tests/fixtures/mergeability_bench/tasks/calculator_addition.json
- tests/fixtures/mergeability_bench/tasks/text_normalization.json
- tests/fixtures/mergeability_bench/candidates/partial_fix_regression.json
- tests/fixtures/mergeability_bench/candidates/text_known_good.json
- tests/fixtures/mergeability_bench/candidates/text_noop.json
- tests/fixtures/mergeability_bench/repos/text_normalization_bug/app/text_ops.py
- tests/fixtures/mergeability_bench/repos/text_normalization_bug/hidden/test_behavior.py
- accept

### Claims

- Prior runtime_changed_files_missing_from_diff rejection is resolved: all deliverable files now in git diff HEAD
- Independent pytest confirms all 5 acceptance tests pass green
- Corrective git-add materially changed index state; not FM-1.3 repetition

### Objections

- None recorded.

### Questions

- None recorded.
